scenarios.description: ExtremeSpeed has +2 priority in Gen 5 (was +1 in Gen 1-4 — ERRATA #7)
scenarios.note: ExtremeSpeed priority increased from +1 to +2 in Gen 5. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen5 — move priority table

scenarios.description: Multi-hit moves use 35/35/15/15 distribution in Gen 5 (changed from Gen 3-4's 37.5/37.5/12.5/12.5 — ERRATA #10)
scenarios.note: Gen 5 changed the multi-hit RNG table from 37.5/37.5/12.5/12.5 to 35/35/15/15 (ERRATA #10). Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen5 — MultiHit distribution

scenarios.description: Sheer Force suppresses secondary effects of moves and also suppresses Life Orb recoil when triggered
scenarios.note: Sheer Force: 1.3x power boost, removes secondary effects, and negates Life Orb recoil when the moved is boosted by Sheer Force. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts SheerForce

scenarios.description: Sturdy at full HP allows a Pokemon to survive any single hit that would KO it, leaving 1 HP (reworked Gen 5)
scenarios.note: Sturdy was reworked in Gen 5 from OHKO-immunity to a Focus Sash-like effect at full HP. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Sturdy

scenarios.description: Consecutive Protect uses: 100% on first use, then halves each subsequent use (50%, 25%, 12.5%...)
scenarios.note: Each consecutive Protect use halves success rate. Attempt 1=100%, 2=50%, 3=25%, 4=12.5%. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts ProtectCheck Gen5

scenarios.description: Magic Bounce reflects status moves back to the sender
scenarios.note: Magic Bounce reflects non-damaging moves that target the opponent. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts MagicBounce

scenarios.description: Prankster gives +1 priority to status moves
scenarios.note: Prankster: status moves used by the bearer gain +1 priority. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Prankster

scenarios.description: Quiver Dance raises Special Attack, Special Defense, and Speed each by +1 stage
scenarios.note: Quiver Dance: +1 SpAtk, +1 SpDef, +1 Speed. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts QuiverDance

scenarios.description: Shell Smash raises Attack, Special Attack, Speed each by +2, but lowers Defense and Special Defense by -1
scenarios.note: Shell Smash: +2 Atk, +2 SpAtk, +2 Speed, -1 Def, -1 SpDef. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts ShellSmash

scenarios.description: In Gen 5, Drizzle/Drought/Sandstorm/Snow Warning summon weather with no turn limit (permanent until overwritten)
scenarios.note: Weather duration was changed in Gen 6 to 5 turns (8 with Damp Rock etc.). In Gen 5 ability-summoned weather is permanent. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen5 weather duration

scenarios.description: Struggle deals 25% of attacker's max HP as recoil in Gen 5 (unchanged from Gen 3-4)
scenarios.note: Gen 5: Struggle recoil = floor(max_hp / 4), same as Gen 3-4. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts StruggleEffect

scenarios.description: Fairy type does not exist in Gen 5; Stealth Rock damage calc uses 17-type chart only
scenarios.note: Fairy type was introduced in Gen 6. Gen 5 has exactly 17 types. Any mechanic relying on Fairy type is invalid in Gen 5. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen5 type chart

scenarios.description: Moxie raises Attack by +1 stage when the bearer directly knocks out an opponent
scenarios.note: Moxie: +1 Attack when the user KOs the target. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Moxie

scenarios.description: Regenerator restores 1/3 of maximum HP when the bearer switches out of battle
scenarios.note: Regenerator: restores floor(max_hp / 3) HP on switch-out. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Regenerator

scenarios.description: Multiscale halves damage taken when the bearer is at full HP
scenarios.note: Multiscale: 0.5x damage when the user is at full HP. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Multiscale

scenarios.description: Sand Force boosts Rock, Steel, and Ground moves by 1.3x in Sandstorm
scenarios.note: Sand Force: 1.3x boost to Rock/Steel/Ground moves in Sandstorm. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts SandForce

scenarios.description: Technician boosts moves with base power 60 or less by 1.5x
scenarios.note: Technician: moves with BP ≤ 60 get 1.5x power boost. Bullet Punch has base power 40. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Technician

scenarios.description: Pixilate, Refrigerate, Aerilate, and Galvanize do not exist in Gen 5 (introduced Gen 6)
scenarios.note: The -ate abilities are Gen 6+ only. Any implementation referencing them must be guarded by gen >= 6. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6+ ability list

scenarios.description: Water Spout at full HP has 150 base power; scales down linearly with current HP
scenarios.note: Water Spout BP = max(1, floor(150 * currentHP / maxHP)). At full HP: 150, at 1 HP: 1. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts WaterSpout

scenarios.description: Water Spout at 1 HP has 1 base power (minimum)
scenarios.note: Water Spout BP = max(1, floor(150 * currentHP / maxHP)). Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts WaterSpout

scenarios.description: Stealth Rock deals 1/2 HP to Charizard (4x weak to Rock)
scenarios.note: Stealth Rock damage = 1/8 * effectiveness. Charizard (Fire/Flying) is 4x weak to Rock. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts StealthRock Gen5

scenarios.description: Evasion-boosting moves like Double Team and Minimize are mechanically valid in Gen 5 (banning is a clause, not a cartridge mechanic)
scenarios.note: Evasion clause bans Double Team/Minimize in competitive play by convention, not cartridge code. The mechanic itself functions normally. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Evasion

scenarios.description: When a move is boosted by Sheer Force, the Life Orb recoil damage is suppressed in addition to secondary effects
scenarios.note: Sheer Force + Life Orb: recoil is suppressed when Sheer Force activates. This is unique to the Sheer Force interaction. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts SheerForce + LifeOrb interaction
